title: Catabrosa scabra
intro: Catabrosa scabra, also known as rough cat's tail, is a perennial grass native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as digestive issues and infections. The bioactive constituents of Catabrosa scabra include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Catabrosa scabra is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to support digestive health and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Catabrosa scabra is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Catabrosa, which is a small group of grasses. Catabrosa scabra is native to Europe and Western Asia. The species is also known as rough sedge or rough brome, although it is not a true brome. It is a perennial grass that grows in damp environments.
